name: BMA
description: Bangkok Metropolitan Administration; the final destination for waste disposal and provider of yellow compression bins.

name: Waste at sorting facility
description: The collective refuse gathered at the central plant before final disposal.

name: Units and offices
description: Departmental or administrative divisions within the university that generate waste.

name: Finance Division
description: The department responsible for processing payments and revenue allocation to units.

name: Revenue from unit waste
description: The financial income generated from waste (split 80/20 between units and the university).

name: N15
description: A private entity that receives cleaned and separated fuel waste (RDF).

name: Separating and cleaning waste
description: The preparatory process required to convert general waste into fuel waste.

name: Infectious waste bins
description: Specialized containers for COVID-19 related materials like ATK kits and masks.

name: E-waste
description: Electronic waste donated by students or staff in exchange for activity hours.

name: Mirror Foundation
description: An organization that receives reusable electronic parts to repair computers for rural schools.

name: Tree debris
description: Organic waste processed into fertilizer.

name: Housekeepers
description: The primary staff responsible for collecting
column4: and moving waste to holding areas.

name: Students
description: The largest group of waste generators; often cited as a factor in management difficulties and overflow.

name: Garden team
description: The staff responsible for collecting external waste around the university using trucks.

name: Waste sorting plant
description: The central facility where collected waste is transported for final sorting.

name: Unsuccessful sorting process
description: A result of having too few staff (3-4 people) relative to the volume of waste.

name: Trash overflow
description: A frequent problem at collection points caused by timing discrepancies or high volume.

name: Working committee
description: The supervisory group that issues warnings and manages waste policy.

name: Gray bins
description: Containers specifically designated for construction waste like iron and cement.

name: Construction waste
description: Includes materials like plywood
column4: and cement from events or renovations.

name: Building PM
description: The project manager or building supervisor who initiates waste pickup requests.

name: Mr. Moss
description: The coordinator responsible for scheduling waste collection rounds.

name: Waste holding cage
description: The physical structure (often described as too small or lacking a roof) where waste is stored.

name: Red Building area
description: A high-traffic location prone to waste overflow due to proximity to markets and schools.

name: Waste quantity
description: The volume of refuse which can be significantly reduced by using reusable equipment.

name: Personnel behavior
description: The human factor (like guessing weights or ignoring signs) that leads to data inaccuracy.

name: Waste weight data
description: The recorded information in notebooks which suffers from extreme inaccuracy due to human error.

name: Equipment damage
description: The physical breaking of bins caused by rough handling by collectors.
